message:
feat(python): six more numpy.polynomial.chebyshev wrappers

Exposes the remaining small-surface Chebyshev helpers via fnp-python as
direct passthroughs, bringing Python-side chebyshev wrapper coverage
from 7 to 13 of the 31 public helpers:

- chebfromroots(roots): inverse of chebroots — builds the Chebyshev
  series of a monic polynomial with the supplied roots.
- chebpow(c, pow, maxpower=16): repeated self-multiplication with the
  maxpower guard wired through.
- chebdiv(c1, c2): returns the (quotient, remainder) tuple.
- chebline(off, scl): 2-coefficient series for off + scl*x.
- chebmulx(c): multiply-by-x closed-form recurrence.
- chebtrim(c, tol=0): drop trailing coefficients with |c| <= tol.

One live-NumPy parity test covers all six: chebfromroots across 0/1/2/3
roots; chebpow at powers 0..3 plus the maxpower-violation ValueError
parity path; chebdiv elementwise on both tuple parts; chebline across
three (off, scl) pairs; chebmulx both against numpy and against the
fnp-python chebmul([0, 1]) identity; chebtrim with default tolerance
and a positive tolerance that trims near-zero tail coefficients.

Same orthogonal-to-panes-2/4 numpy.polynomial.chebyshev domain as
c23d71e and the chebyshev arithmetic triad landed in 464fd84.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
